IPOVALL.description: Phase-1 solved overall poverty rate from the in-model distribution identity block. It responds to unemployment, the aggregate transfer-resource bridge, and a parsimonious deviation basis that separates UI-heavy support from federal-versus-state/local transfer mix shifts.

IPOVCH.description: Phase-1 solved child poverty rate from the in-model distribution identity block. It is built from the overall poverty state plus a child-specific gap identity that now shares the aggregate transfer bridge and the same parsimonious transfer-mix deviation basis.

IGINIHH.description: Phase-1 solved household Gini from the in-model distribution identity block. It is currently a provisional reduced-form inequality diagnostic rather than a publication-ready headline, and in the current relief runs it can move differently from poverty outcomes.

IMEDRINC.description: Phase-1 solved median-style household resource proxy from the in-model distribution identity block. It is currently a provisional disposable-resource-and-unemployment diagnostic rather than a validated median-income forecast; prefer `RYDPC` for phase-1 headline household resources.

UB.description: Stock Fair unemployment-benefit transfer flow used as the UI transmission channel in phase-1 runs.

TRGH.description: Stock Fair federal household-transfer flow used as the broad federal-transfer transmission channel in phase-1 runs.

TRSH.description: Stock Fair state/local household-transfer flow used as the state/local transfer transmission channel in phase-1 runs. In stock construction it is tied to state/local social-benefit payments rather than a clean Social Security-only series.

YD.description: Stock Fair disposable income used as a key household-resource bridge in the phase-1 export.

TRLOWZ.description: Derived transfer-resource bridge equal to household transfer flows scaled by population and prices. Used in the phase-1 poverty and Gini identities.

RYDPC.description: Derived real disposable-income-per-capita resource measure. This is the preferred phase-1 public household-resource signal.

LGDPR.description: Derived log real GDP state retained for expert inspection and historical comparison. It is no longer the preferred phase-1 household-resource driver.

LRYDPC.short_name: Log Real Disposable Income Per Capita
LRYDPC.description: Latent log real disposable-income-per-capita state used in the provisional phase-1 median-style resource identity.

LPOVALL.description: Latent overall-poverty state solved inside the phase-1 identity block before logistic transformation to `IPOVALL`.

LPOVCHGAP.description: Latent child-poverty gap state solved inside the phase-1 identity block before combination with `LPOVALL`.

LGINIHH.description: Latent household-inequality state solved inside the phase-1 identity block before logistic transformation to `IGINIHH`.

LMEDINC.description: Latent median-style household resource state solved inside the phase-1 identity block before exponentiation to `IMEDRINC`.

IWGAP1050.description: Estimated wealth-share gap between the top 10 percent and the bottom 50 percent. Higher values imply greater wealth concentration.

IWGAP150.description: Estimated wealth-share gap between the top 1 percent and the bottom 50 percent. Higher values imply greater wealth concentration at the top.

ITRCOMP.description: Composite transfer-policy input used by the inequality overlay. It summarizes the net support signal from transfer-side policy channels.

IUIBEN.description: Unemployment-insurance benefits input for the inequality overlay. Higher values indicate more generous UI support in the scenario path.

ISSBEN.description: Social Security benefits input for the inequality overlay. This series shifts the policy support path used in scenario comparisons.

ISNAP.description: SNAP support input for the inequality overlay. Higher values represent stronger food-assistance support in the scenario path.

ICRDCMP.description: Composite credit-conditions input for the inequality overlay. It summarizes credit-side pressure and support conditions in the scenario path.

IHHNW.description: Household net worth input for the inequality overlay. It is mainly used to shape wealth-distribution outcomes in scenario comparisons.

IHOMEQ.description: Home equity input for the inequality overlay. It captures the housing-wealth channel used by the wealth-gap equations.

IFFUNDS.description: Federal funds rate input used by the inequality overlay as a monetary-policy sensitivity channel.

UR.description: Stock Fair unemployment rate series used as a macro driver and shown for bridge context in the public explorer.

GDPR.description: Stock Fair real GDP series used as a macro driver and shown as context in the public explorer.

PCY.description: Stock Fair real GDP growth rate shown for macro context in the public explorer.

PIEF.description: Stock Fair private fixed investment series shown for macro context in the public explorer.

9.label: Overall poverty identity
9.formula: UBZ = (UB-UBBAR)/UBSTD; TRGHZ = (TRGH-TRGHBAR)/TRGHSTD; TRSHZ = (TRSH-TRSHBAR)/TRSHSTD; UIDEV = UBZ - 0.5*(TRGHZ+TRSHZ); GHSHDV = TRGHZ - TRSHZ; LPOVALL = PV0 + PVU*UR + PVT*TRLOWZ + PVUI*UIDEV + PVGH*GHSHDV; IPOVALL = EXP(LPOVALL)/(1+EXP(LPOVALL))

19.label: Child poverty identity
19.formula: LPOVCHGAP = CG0 + CGU*UR + CGT*TRLOWZ + CGUI*UIDEV + CGGH*GHSHDV; IPOVCH = EXP(LPOVALL+LPOVCHGAP)/(1+EXP(LPOVALL+LPOVCHGAP))

20.label: Household Gini identity
20.formula: LGINIHH = GN0 + GNU*UR + GNT*TRLOWZ; IGINIHH = EXP(LGINIHH)/(1+EXP(LGINIHH))

21.label: Median-style resource proxy identity
21.formula: LRYDPC = LOG(RYDPC); LMEDINC = MD0 + MDR*LRYDPC + MDU*UR; IMEDRINC = EXP(LMEDINC)
